Patch Set 3:

James,

Could you set CIT_READ to 1 and see if the warning goes away? I think that gcc is assuming to much here.

Patch Set 3:

gcc is right. An unsigned value will never be less than zero and a zero value is valid. There is no point in that test. Does changing CIT_READ to 1 have negative affects on the code ?  Perhaps Bobi or Jinshan have a good answer to that. Why do you want to preserve the format of the test so badly?

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

> gcc is right. .. so badly?

I don't see a side-effect by changing CIT_READ to 1. The current patch works for me too.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3:

There's no guarantee that enum cl_io_type will be unsigned. So gcc is wrong here. The warning code is not implemented correctly.

Patch Set 3:

Did you try changing CIT_READ to 1? Did the warning go away?

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

Assuming we want to get rid of this warning, I think setting CIT_READ = 1 (and having it be the lowest numbered enum cl_io_type constant) is the cleanest way to do so.

At least the grant underflow seems worthwhile to fix in my mind.
